cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about why the author said a loud "thank you" to the doorman. The options are A, B, C, D. Let me go back to the story to find the clues. The author explains that her kids had been rude, commenting on the man's smell. She wanted to set a good example. Later, when she went back to get the car-sick pills, the doorman was opening the door again, and she said a loud thank you. Then she told her family about his situation and ordered food for him. The main point she makes at the end is about teaching her children by example and choosing to show gratitude. So when she said "thank you", it was likely part of her effort to demonstrate gratitude despite the kids' initial rudeness. The purpose was to give them a lesson in gratitude. Option A says she wanted to give the children a lesson of gratitude. Option D is about polite manner. But the story emphasizes that she wanted to set a good example, so A seems correct.
